items.title: Plotly API layers
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  df["pandas DataFrame / arrays"]
  px["Plotly Express — px.scatter, px.line, ..."]
  go["Graph Objects — go.Scatter, go.Figure"]
  fig["Figure — traces + layout"]
  out["show / write_html / Dash Graph"]
  df --> px
  df --> go
  px --> fig
  go --> fig
  fig --> out
items.caption: Express is a convenience layer over Graph Objects; both produce the same interactive Figure type.

items.title: Typical ML interactive plotting workflow
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  data["Features, metrics, coordinates"] --> choose["Express or GO"]
  choose --> build["Create traces + layout"]
  build --> hover["hover_data / hovertemplate"]
  hover --> share["show or write_html or Dash"]
items.caption: Hover configuration is the main ML-specific step between building traces and sharing the figure.

items.title: When Express vs Graph Objects
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  eda["DataFrame EDA"] --> express["Plotly Express"]
  custom["Custom traces / mixed types"] --> graphobj["Graph Objects"]
  express --> same["Interactive Figure"]
  graphobj --> same
items.caption: Express for speed on tidy tables; Graph Objects when trace-level control or subplot composition demands it.
